papers.title_en: Can Open-Source LLM Agents Replace Static Application Security Testing Tools? An Empirical Assessment
papers.authors: Derek Yohn, Luke Flancher, Mirajul Islam, Khaled Slhoub
papers.evidence: 评估大模型智能体在应用安全测试中的能力，属于网络能力边界探索
papers.tldr: 随着大语言模型技术发展，开源LLM代理被视为有望提升安全测试效率，但其在专业SAST场景的适用性仍缺乏实证。本研究选取三款通过Ollama托管的通用开源模型构建代理，与成熟工具Bandit进行基准测试，记录精确率、召回率、假阳数并计算综合评分。结果显示，开源LLM代理在实际SAST扫描中误报严重、检出率不足，远未达到生产环境要求。研究有力反驳了开源LLM可即时替代静态安全测试工具的观点，为AI安全应用划定了当前能力边界。
papers.abstract_en: This paper explores the value of agentic AI tools for cybersecurity purposes. We evaluate the efficacy of a general-purpose GenAI Large Language Model- (GenAI-) based agent when powered by three different Ollama-hosted general-purpose open source models. We assess each agent's performance using precision, recall, false positive count, and a calculated composite score based upon the interplay of the captured metrics, against the baseline performance of an existing, vetted Static Application Security Testing (SAST) tool, Bandit. Our findings refute the notion that a modern open-source GenAI LLM-based agent is currently suitable for the specialized task of SAST scanning under realistic conditions.

papers.title_en: AgentTrust: A Self-Improving Trust Layer for AI-Agent Actions
papers.evidence: 为AI智能体行为设计的信任层区分词汇与语义威胁，直接应对LLM智能体的安全问题。
papers.tldr: AI代理需信任层判断操作安全性，但传统固定规则无法应对表面相似但意图不同的语义威胁。AgentTrust提出按威胁类型区分的双存储系统：LLM法官自学习，为词汇威胁蒸馏确定性规则，为语义威胁构建带验证的RAG记忆。规则准确率从48%提升至83.6-85.2%，语义准确率提高13个百分点，在线重放中零错误阻止良性操作，法官调用率下降而准确率上升，实现自进化、低成本、高安全的信任层。
papers.abstract_en: AI agents increasingly take consequential actions -- shell commands, cloud operations, and arbitrary tool-calls -- so a trust layer must decide, per action, whether to allow, warn, block, or escalate. We argue that the right way to reason about such a layer is by threat type. Lexical (fixed-signature) threats, where danger lives in a stable token, are decidable by deterministic rules; semantic (intent-dependent) threats, where a benign and a malicious action share the same surface, are out of reach for rules by construction. We make this concrete with a negative proof: a determined, hand-authored cloud rule pack lifts held-out accuracy only 48 to 56% overall and moves the semantic categories by 0pp (data_db 29 to 29, observability 59 to 59, supply_chain 50 to 50), while a strong LLM judge carries exactly those categories. We give the judge a self-learning capability: on a corpus that is mainly semantic attacks it nearly doubles rule accuracy (48% to 83.6-85.2%) with near-zero false-blocks, and this holds across two model providers. We turn this into a self-improving dual-store system: the judge distills a growing deterministic rule floor on lexical threats (cheaper over time) and feeds a guarded RAG memory on semantic threats (a verdict-cache fails -- surface-twins collapse to ~58% -- so a corroboration guard lifts semantic accuracy +13pp, 70 to 84). The result is what sets AgentTrust v2 apart from its static v1 predecessor: a trust layer that self-evolves from its own stream of decisions -- cheaper on the lexical class (it distils its own rules) and smarter on the semantic class (it accrues guarded precedent), while never hard-blocking a benign action. An end-to-end online replay shows the judge-call rate falling (50% to 44%) and judge-domain accuracy rising (71% to 80%), with 0 benign hard-blocks across 45,000 actions.

papers.title_en: Sample-Efficient LLM-Based Detection of Malicious Web Server Logs with Forensically Explainable Reasoning
papers.authors: Bernhard Kneip, Nhien-An Le-Khac, Hong-Hanh Nguyen-Le
papers.evidence: 基于大模型的高精度恶意日志检测，展示网络取证能力
papers.tldr: Web服务器日志取证分析要求高精度检测与可解释性以满足法律需求。为此，提出CEF-Log，一种上下文增强的少样本思维链提示策略，通过结构化五步推理模板嵌入专家调查方法，使LLM学习如何分析而非记忆模式。在CSIC 2010数据集上仅需4个示例即达F1=0.99，样本效率较其他提示方法提升10倍。同时构建包含真实攻击和多步序列的新数据集ForenWebLog，生成的解释可追踪且准确，解决了传统机器学习黑箱问题。
papers.abstract_en: Forensic analysis of web server logs demands both accurate detection and human-readable explanations that can satisfy legal requirements. We present CEF-Log, a context-enhanced few-shot chain-of-thought prompting strategy for Large Language Models that addresses this dual requirement. CEF-Log embeds expert investigative methodology through a structured five-step reasoning template, enabling the model to learn \textit{how} to analyze logs rather than \textit{what} patterns to memorize. Experimental evaluation demonstrates that CEF-Log achieves an F1-score of 0.99 on the CSIC 2010 dataset using only four examples while providing a $10\times$ improvement in sample efficiency compared to other prompting-based methods. We also introduce ForenWebLog, a new dataset that incorporates real-world attacks and multi-step attack sequences for comprehensive evaluation. Qualitative analysis confirms that CEF-Log generates traceable, accurate explanations suitable for forensic documentation, addressing the critical "black-box" limitation of traditional machine learning approaches.

papers.title_en: VESTA: A Fully Automated Scenario Generation and Safety Evaluation Framework for LLM Agents
papers.authors: Lu Jia, Haibo Tong, Feifei Zhao, Jindong Li, Dongqi Liang, Ping Wu, Qian Zhang, Yi Zeng
papers.evidence: 自动化生成LLM智能体的多样化安全风险场景，可适配用于网络犯罪场景测试
papers.tldr: 大语言模型（LLM）智能体日益具备记忆、工具使用和环境交互能力，安全风险多样化，但现有评估依赖手动静态场景，难以覆盖执行中的动态风险。为此提出VESTA全自动框架，基于五类风险生成1072个可度量场景并构建评估流程。在两种权限下评测12个LLM智能体，平均ASR达47.1%，部分模型超70%。结果表明过程级可执行评估对理解与改进智能体安全至关重要。
papers.abstract_en: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly evolving from simple text-based interaction systems into LLM agents that can maintain memory, use tools, access external environments, and execute tasks. As their capabilities and autonomy expand, the safety risks they face also become more diverse. Existing evaluations often rely on manually written scenarios, static prompts, or final-output judgments, making it difficult to capture the diverse risks that agents may face during task execution. We introduce VESTA, a fully automated scenario generation and safety evaluation framework for LLM agents. Based on five risk dimensions, VESTA instantiaes abstract and diverse safety risks in real-world task execution into 1,072 measurable evaluation scenarios. Using the automated evaluation pipeline, 12 LLM agents are evaluated under two authority contexts. The results show that current agents still face substantial behavioral safety risks during task execution, with an average ASR of 47.1% and several models exceeding 70%. These findings demonstrate the importance of executable, process-level evaluation for understanding and improving LLM agent safety.

papers.title_en: Autonomous Incident Resolution at Hyperscale: An Agentic AI Architecture for Network Operations
papers.evidence: 用于自主网络事件处理的多智能体编排，与多智能体网络操作相关。
papers.tldr: 随着云网络基础设施规模扩大，故障量、速度和复杂度剧增，传统人工事件响应已无法满足需求。为此，本文提出一种多智能体AI架构，通过分层智能体协作、标准化工具调用及运行手册知识编码，实现事件的自主检测、诊断与修复，并嵌入渐进自主、安全边界与闭环验证机制。该架构在主要云提供商生产环境部署，常见事件类别自主解决率超90%，同时分层授权与回滚保障安全。该工作证实了智能体AI在超大规模网络运维中的可行性，并为自主事件管理提供了重要经验。
papers.abstract_en: Cloud network infrastructure at hyperscale presents unique operational challenges where traditional human-driven incident response cannot keep pace with the volume, velocity, and complexity of failures. This paper presents an agentic AI architecture for autonomous incident resolution in large-scale network operations. Our system employs a multi-agent orchestration framework where specialized AI agents collaborate to detect, diagnose, and remediate network incidents without human intervention. We describe the architectural principles, including hierarchical agent decomposition, skills-based tool invocation via standardized protocols, structured knowledge encoding from operational runbooks, progressive autonomy with safety boundaries, and closed-loop verification. The architecture has been deployed in production at a major cloud provider, demonstrating that agentic AI systems can achieve autonomous resolution rates exceeding 90% for common incident categories while maintaining safety guarantees through layered authorization and rollback mechanisms. We discuss design tradeoffs, failure modes, and lessons learned from operating autonomous AI agents at scale.

papers.title_en: Decentralized Multi-Agent Systems with Shared Context
papers.evidence: 提出一个可用于模拟网络犯罪活动的去中心化多智能体框架
papers.tldr: 现有多智能体系统依赖集中协调，任务增多时控制器成为瓶颈。DeLM采用去中心化架构，通过共享验证上下文和任务队列实现智能体异步协作。在SWE-bench Verified上，DeLM跨指标最佳，最高提升10.5个百分点且成本减半；在LongBench-v2多文档问答中，平均准确率领先，最高提升5.7个百分点。该框架为去中心化推理扩展提供了高效方案。
papers.abstract_en: Multi-agent systems (MAS) can scale large language model reasoning at test time by decomposing complex problems into parallel subtasks. However, most existing MAS rely on centralized orchestration, where a main agent assigns work, collects outputs, and merges results. As the number of subtasks grows, this controller becomes a communication and integration bottleneck. We propose Decentralized Language Models (DeLM), a MAS framework that decentralizes coordination through parallel agents, a shared verified context, and a task queue. Agents asynchronously claim subtasks, read accumulated progress, perform local reasoning, and write back compact verified updates. The shared context acts as a common communication substrate, enabling agents to build on one another's verified progress without routing every update through a central controller. Empirically, DeLM improves both software-engineering test-time scaling and long-context reasoning. On SWE-bench Verified, DeLM achieves the best performance across Avg.@1, Pass@2, and Pass@4, with gains of up to 10.5 percentage points over the strongest baseline, while reducing cost per task by roughly 50%. On LongBench-v2 Multi-Doc QA, DeLM achieves the highest average accuracy across four frontier model families, improving over the strongest baseline by up to 5.7 percentage points. The code is available on our project website at https://yuzhenmao.github.io/DeLM/.
